column1: # Hourly restart tick. The concurrency group below means only one bot

column2: so while a 5h50m run is alive these extra ticks

column1: # are harmless no-ops — but the moment a run ends (cleanly at 5h50m) or

column2: the next hourly tick restarts it within <=1h instead of

column1: # waiting up to 6h. GitHub's scheduler is best-effort/often delayed
# hourly is far more resilient than every-6-hours for "never stop".
# Only one bot run at a time per fork. Wait for the running one to exit
# cleanly (it self-terminates at 5h50m) before starting the next.
# cancel-in-progress: false prevents a cron tick from killing a bot
# REQUIRED so the workflow can git-push state updates with GITHUB_TOKEN.
# Persist creds so the bot can `git push` state updates back during the run.
# `git push --force-with-lease` collapsed all prior commits into a

column2: wiping every code change that landed during the run.

column1: # Full history makes amend-and-rebase preserve ancestors.
- name: Install npm deps (if package.json present)
if [ -f package.json ]; then npm ci --no-audit --no-fund; fi
echo "::error::KALSHI_API_KEY secret is missing. Set it in your fork's Settings → Secrets → Actions
column2: or click Start in the dashboard which pushes it for you."

column1: echo "::error::KALSHI_PRIVATE_KEY secret is missing. Same fix as above."
- name: Write Kalshi RSA private key to disk
# Pass the secret in an env var instead of interpolating directly
# into the shell. Even though `secrets.*` substitution is masked
# this prevents any quoting/escaping surprises in the PEM.
# Sanity check: BEGIN line should be present
head -1 kalshi_private_key.pem | grep -q "BEGIN " || { echo "::error::PEM does not start with BEGIN — paste the FULL key including header & footer"; exit 1; }
# Default LIVE — cron restarts (no input) must stay live
# Optional analytics sink — leave blank to skip.
# Run for up to 5h50m so the next cron restart picks up where this leaves off.
# Smoke check: if the bot exits within 15s it probably crashed on
# so the Actions run shows red instead of a silent green exit.
echo "::error::Bot exited within 15s (code $rc). Check for missing scan loop or startup crash."
# Bot is alive — wait for it to finish its full run.
